attractions.name: Mendenhall Glacier & Visitor Center
attractions.season: Visitor center daily 9-6 May-Sep; Fri-Sat 10-4 Nov-Mar
attractions.blurb: Lake-terminating glacier 12 mi from downtown Juneau, with a USFS visitor center, viewing decks and three short trails.
attractions.tip: Suicide Basin outburst floods hit the Mendenhall River most Jul-Aug; check NWS Juneau before booking riverside lodging.

attractions.name: Nugget Falls Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free late Apr-Oct; icy in winter
attractions.blurb: Flat 2 mi round-trip gravel path from the Mendenhall visitor center to a 377 ft waterfall beside the glacier face.
attractions.tip: Go before 10am or after 3pm; cruise buses saturate it midday. The beach at the falls is windy and much colder.

attractions.name: West Glacier Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free Jun-Oct; upper section holds snow into June
attractions.blurb: 3.4 mi one-way trail up the glacier's west flank to bedrock overlooks; the local route to real ice-level views.
attractions.tip: Scrambles onto the ice and into caves beyond the marked end are unroped and have killed people. Stop at the rock knob.

attractions.name: Mount Roberts Tramway
attractions.season: Early May to late Sep; closed in winter
attractions.blurb: Aerial tram rising 1,800 ft from the Juneau cruise docks to an alpine lodge, nature center and ridge trail network.
attractions.tip: The ticket is valid all day: ride up, hike the Alpine Loop toward Gastineau Peak, then come down in evening light.

attractions.name: Juneau
attractions.season: Year-round; tourism season May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Alaska's capital, with no road to anywhere else: compact waterfront downtown, five cruise berths, gateway to the icefield.
attractions.tip: Up to five megaships dock daily Jun-Aug. Check the CBJ ship calendar and plan glacier trips on a light-ship day.

attractions.name: Alaska State Museum
attractions.season: Year-round; daily in summer, fewer days in winter
attractions.blurb: The state's flagship museum: Tlingit, Haida and Unangax collections, Russian-America artifacts, a Fresnel lighthouse lens.
attractions.tip: Best rainy-day option in Juneau and a 10-minute walk from the cruise docks; go when your glacier flight cancels.

attractions.name: Sealaska Heritage - Walter Soboleff Building
attractions.season: Year-round; longer summer hours
attractions.blurb: Tlingit, Haida and Tsimshian cultural center downtown with a full clan house, carved cedar screen and contemporary art.
attractions.tip: The exterior Lovers' Embrace glass panels are free to view; the shop here is a reliable source of authentic Native art.

attractions.name: Alaska State Capitol
attractions.season: Free guided tours daily May-Sep; self-guided otherwise
attractions.blurb: The 1931 former federal building that serves as Alaska's capitol; free 30-minute guided tours run through the summer.
attractions.tip: It looks like a brick office block, not a domed capitol. Ten minutes uphill is the Governor's Mansion for photos.

attractions.name: Last Chance Mining Museum & Perseverance Trail
attractions.season: Museum mid-May to mid-Sep; trail Jun-Oct
attractions.blurb: Compressor house of the old Alaska-Juneau gold mine at the end of Basin Road, plus the Perseverance Trail trailhead.
attractions.tip: Walk Basin Road from downtown in 25 minutes; the Gold Creek gorge and the flume walk are better than the museum.

attractions.name: Juneau Icefield helicopter tour & glacier dog camp
attractions.season: Helicopter tours mid-May to mid-Sep; sled-dog camp Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: 1,500 sq mi icefield behind Juneau; helicopters land on Norris, Herbert or Mendenhall ice, some at a summer sled-dog camp.
attractions.tip: Weather scrubs a real share of flights. Book the first morning of your Juneau stay so you keep a rebooking day.

attractions.name: Taku Glacier Lodge & Wilderness Salmon Bake
attractions.season: Floatplane trips late May to mid-Sep only
attractions.blurb: 1923 log lodge facing Taku Glacier, reachable only by floatplane; grilled king salmon and habituated black bears outside.
attractions.tip: The flight over five Taku Inlet glaciers justifies the price alone. Roughly ten flights a day, so book weeks ahead.

attractions.name: Auke Bay whale watching (Stephens Passage)
attractions.season: Humpbacks mid-May to Sep; peak Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Small boats leave Statter Harbor for humpback bubble-net feeding, orcas and Steller sea lions around Shelter Island.
attractions.tip: Most operators guarantee a whale sighting or refund. Afternoon departures usually get calmer water in Auke Bay.

attractions.name: Macaulay Salmon Hatchery
attractions.season: Fish running mid-Jun to Sep; tours May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Working hatchery with a fish ladder you watch from above plus saltwater touch tanks of local sea life.
attractions.tip: Only interesting once salmon are actually running, roughly late June onward; easy add-on driving to Mendenhall.

attractions.name: Alaskan Brewing Company
attractions.season: Tasting room open year-round
attractions.blurb: Brewery behind Alaskan Amber, founded 1986, with a tasting room and short production tour in the Lemon Creek area.
attractions.tip: It sits in an industrial park 20 min from downtown, so only worth it if you already have a car for Mendenhall.

attractions.name: Glacier Gardens Rainforest Adventure
attractions.season: Open May to late Sep
attractions.blurb: Hillside rainforest garden known for upside-down spruce root flower towers; golf-cart tour to a 580 ft channel overlook.
attractions.tip: The overlook is the payoff, not the flowers. Skip it if time is tight and ride Mount Roberts Tramway instead.

attractions.name: Eaglecrest Ski Area
attractions.season: Ski season Dec-Apr, weather dependent; summer trail access
attractions.blurb: Juneau's community ski area on Douglas Island: four lifts, sea-level-to-2,600 ft terrain, maritime snow and frequent rain.
attractions.tip: Check the lift and season status before planning around it; the long-promised gondola project has still not opened.

attractions.name: Auke Bay Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Sailings year-round; most frequent May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Juneau's Alaska Marine Highway dock sits 14 mi from downtown; no state ferry ever calls at the cruise piers.
attractions.tip: Budget a $40 cab or pre-book a shuttle. The terminal opens about two hours before sailing and has no real food.

attractions.name: Glacier Bay National Park - Bartlett Cove
attractions.season: Park open all year; lodge, visitor center and day boat mid-May to early Sep
attractions.blurb: Park headquarters, lodge, campground and dock - the only road-accessible corner of a 3.3-million-acre park with no highway.
attractions.tip: Fly Juneau-Gustavus in 15 min or take the summer Alaska Airlines jet; the 10-mile park road is the only road here.
attractions.duration_note: 1-3 days independent; a passing day for cruise ships

attractions.name: Glacier Bay day boat to Tarr Inlet
attractions.aka: High Speed Catamaran Glacier & Wildlife Tour
attractions.season: Daily mid-May to early Sep only
attractions.blurb: Eight-hour catamaran from the Glacier Bay Lodge dock, about 130 mi round trip to Margerie and Grand Pacific glaciers.
attractions.tip: It is the only scheduled day boat in the park and it sells out; book with lodging through Glacier Bay Lodge months ahead.

attractions.name: Margerie Glacier
attractions.season: Reached by ship or day boat late May-Sep
attractions.cost: Included in cruise or day boat
attractions.blurb: Mile-wide, 250 ft tidewater face at the head of Tarr Inlet - the glacier cruise ships hold in front of in Glacier Bay.
attractions.tip: Ships stop 15-30 min. Stand outside on deck: calving is heard as a crack seconds before anything is visible.

attractions.name: Gustavus
attractions.season: Most services mid-May to mid-Sep; jet service Jun 6-Aug 30, 2026
attractions.blurb: 450-person town on a glacial outwash plain: the airstrip, lodges, ferry landing and grocery that make Glacier Bay possible.
attractions.tip: Gustavus and Bartlett Cove are 10 mi apart with no taxi rank; arrange lodge transfers before you fly in.

attractions.name: Point Adolphus
attractions.season: Humpbacks concentrate Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: Tidal upwelling off the north tip of Chichagof Island that concentrates feeding humpbacks; a classic Icy Strait target.
attractions.tip: Better whale odds than most Juneau trips. Run from Gustavus or Hoonah; it is a long open crossing if you are kayaking.

attractions.name: Icy Strait Point
attractions.season: Cruise season May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Tlingit-owned cruise destination built on a 1912 cannery: two gondolas, the 5,330 ft ZipRider, and whale-watch boats.
attractions.tip: The zipline sells out onboard. The free shoreline trail into Hoonah village is the underrated half of the day.

attractions.name: Tracy Arm Fjord
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Tracy Arm-Fords Terror Wilderness
attractions.season: Closed to cruise ships and tour boats for the 2026 season
attractions.blurb: Narrow granite fjord to the Sawyer glaciers; an Aug 2025 landslide and megatsunami has kept vessels out through 2026.
attractions.tip: Holland America, Carnival, Royal Caribbean, MSC and Allen Marine all swapped 2026 sailings to Endicott Arm instead.

attractions.name: Endicott Arm & Dawes Glacier
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Tracy Arm-Fords Terror Wilderness
attractions.season: Ice permitting, May-Sep
attractions.cost: $180-260 day boat from Juneau; included on ships
attractions.blurb: Iceberg-filled 30 mi fjord off Stephens Passage ending at the calving face of Dawes Glacier; 2026's Tracy Arm substitute.
attractions.tip: Day boats from Juneau run about nine hours. Brash ice sometimes stops vessels short - ask the turnaround policy first.

attractions.name: Sitka
attractions.season: Year-round; tours and museums May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Former Russian-America capital, the only major SE town facing the open Pacific; Tlingit history, volcano view, walkable core.
attractions.tip: Ships use the Sitka Sound Cruise Terminal 5 mi out of town; the shuttle costs extra and queues, so budget the time.

attractions.name: Sitka National Historical Park
attractions.season: Trails year-round; visitor center daily May-Sep, reduced in winter
attractions.blurb: Alaska's oldest federal park: site of the 1804 Tlingit-Russian battle, with a mile of rainforest trail lined with totem poles.
attractions.tip: Free and 15 min on foot from downtown. Native carvers and metalsmiths work in the Cultural Center studios most weekdays.

attractions.name: Alaska Raptor Center
attractions.season: Daily May-Sep; limited hours in winter
attractions.blurb: Bald eagle rehabilitation hospital with a 20,000 sq ft flight-training center and resident non-releasable raptors at eye level.
attractions.tip: Flight demonstrations run through the day. It backs onto the Indian River Trail, so pair it with the totem park walk.

attractions.name: Fortress of the Bear
attractions.season: Daily May-Sep; call ahead in winter
attractions.blurb: Orphaned brown and black bears living in converted pulp-mill clarifier tanks 5 mi out Sawmill Creek Road; sightings guaranteed.
attractions.tip: This is a rescue, not wild viewing. Great for kids and rain days, but Anan or Pack Creek is the real experience.

attractions.name: St. Michael's Cathedral
attractions.season: Open when ships are in, roughly May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Russian Orthodox cathedral standing in the middle of Sitka's main street; a 1966 fire replica holding rescued 19th-c icons.
attractions.tip: The icons are the originals, saved by townspeople from the fire. The Russian Bishop's House two blocks away is 1842 fabric.

attractions.name: Castle Hill
attractions.season: Year-round; paved path
attractions.blurb: Paved switchback to the knoll where Russia formally transferred Alaska to the United States on 18 October 1867.
attractions.tip: Two-minute climb, best free view in Sitka. It faces west over the sound and Mount Edgecumbe, so go at sunset.

attractions.name: Mount Edgecumbe
attractions.season: Trail snow-free Jul-Sep; boat drop required
attractions.blurb: Dormant 3,201 ft stratovolcano on Kruzof Island, the cone that defines Sitka's skyline; 6.7 mi trail to the crater rim.
attractions.tip: Needs a boat drop at Fred's Creek and a full clear day. Most visitors simply photograph it from Castle Hill.

attractions.name: Skagway
attractions.season: Cruise season May-Sep; largely shut in winter
attractions.blurb: Gold-rush boomtown at the head of Lynn Canal; six blocks of restored 1898 false-front buildings inside a national park.
attractions.tip: The big Railroad Dock has been closed to pedestrians since the 2022 rockslides - expect a shuttle or tender, plus 30 min.
attractions.duration_note: a ship day, or 2 nights to reach the Yukon

attractions.name: White Pass & Yukon Route Railroad
attractions.season: Mid-May to late Sep; up to four daily departures Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: 1898 narrow-gauge line climbing 2,865 ft in 20 mi over trestles and through tunnels to White Pass; Summit run is 3 hours.
attractions.tip: Sit on the left going up for Bridal Veil Falls and Dead Horse Gulch. A passport is required for anything past the summit.

attractions.name: Klondike Gold Rush NHP Visitor Center
attractions.season: Daily early May to late Sep; winter by appointment
attractions.blurb: Housed in the 1898 WP&YR depot: gold-rush films, the 'ton of goods' outfit display, and free ranger walking tours of town.
attractions.tip: Walking tours at 9, 10, 11am and 2, 3pm cap at 30 people; free tickets go same-morning at the desk, so arrive early.

attractions.name: Chilkoot Trail (Dyea trailhead)
attractions.season: US side hikeable Jun to mid-Sep; permits open ~Apr 15
attractions.cost: NPS backcountry camping permit (reserve on Recreation.gov)
attractions.blurb: The 33 mi gold-rush route to Bennett Lake. In 2026 the border at Chilkoot Pass is still shut - out-and-back on each side only.
attractions.tip: Day-hike Dyea to Finnegan's Point (4.9 mi) if you cannot through-hike; US camping permits open around April 15.

attractions.name: Dyea Townsite & Slide Cemetery
attractions.season: Dyea Road generally open May-Oct; unpaved
attractions.blurb: Ghost townsite 9 mi from Skagway: wharf pilings, a few ruins and the cemetery for the 1898 Palm Sunday avalanche dead.
attractions.tip: Dyea Road is steep single-lane with blind corners; some rental contracts forbid it. Taxis run roughly $40 each way.

attractions.name: Gold Rush Cemetery
attractions.season: Year-round; muddy in shoulder season
attractions.blurb: Two miles up the valley: the graves of Soapy Smith and Frank Reid from the 1898 shootout, with Reid Falls just behind.
attractions.tip: Walkable in about 40 minutes from town along the river. Keep going five minutes past the graves for the waterfall.

attractions.name: South Klondike Highway to White Pass summit
attractions.season: Open and plowed year-round; alpine best Jun-Sep
attractions.cost: Free; passport required to cross into BC
attractions.blurb: Fourteen miles from tidewater to the 3,292 ft White Pass summit and the BC border: alpine tundra, waterfalls, Tormented Valley.
attractions.tip: The only road out of Skagway. Canadian customs sits 8 mi inside BC at Fraser, not at the border itself.

attractions.name: Haines
attractions.season: Year-round; ferry and road access all year
attractions.blurb: Road-connected Lynn Canal town 15 mi from Skagway by water and 359 mi by road; few ships, real working community.
attractions.tip: The seasonal fast ferry from Skagway takes about 45 min and is the easy way to add Haines to a cruise or road trip.

attractions.name: Alaska Chilkat Bald Eagle Preserve
attractions.season: Eagles present year-round; the 3,000+ gathering is Oct to early Dec
attractions.blurb: 48,000-acre preserve where warm upwelling keeps a late chum run open, drawing the world's largest gathering of bald eagles.
attractions.tip: Peak is early-to-mid November, not summer. Four pullouts sit at Haines Hwy Miles 18-21; Mile 19 has boardwalk and scopes.
attractions.duration_note: 2 hours by car; half-day by raft

attractions.name: Fort William H. Seward
attractions.season: Year-round; galleries and carving shed mainly May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Alaska's first permanent army post (1904), now a landmark district of white officers' houses around a grass parade ground.
attractions.tip: Walk the parade loop and stop at Alaska Indian Arts, the Tlingit carving shed in the old post hospital building.

attractions.name: Hammer Museum
attractions.season: Weekdays May-Sep; closed off season
attractions.blurb: One-room Main Street museum holding 2,000+ hammers, from Roman nail-pullers to whaling tools, with a giant hammer outside.
attractions.tip: Closed weekends and outside summer. Thirty minutes is enough, but it is the best small-museum gimmick in Southeast.

attractions.name: Haines Highway over Chilkat Pass
attractions.season: Open and plowed year-round; alpine best Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: 146 mi byway from tidewater over 3,510 ft Chilkat Pass to Haines Junction, Yukon; you clear treeline within 40 miles.
attractions.tip: US customs at Mile 40 closes overnight in shoulder season. Fuel in Haines - there is nothing for 100 mi past the border.

attractions.name: Wrangell
attractions.season: Year-round; tours May-Sep
attractions.blurb: One of the least cruise-touched towns in Southeast: Tlingit fort site, Stikine River gateway, working fishing harbor.
attractions.tip: Only small ships call. Get here on the state ferry or the Alaska Airlines milk run; two nights unlocks Anan and the Stikine.

attractions.name: Chief Shakes Island & Tribal House
attractions.season: Island open year-round; house interior on request or ship days
attractions.blurb: Footbridge islet in Wrangell harbor with a rebuilt Tlingit clan house and a stand of replica and original totem poles.
attractions.tip: Interior opens on request through the Wrangell Cooperative Association; the poles and bridge are always free to walk.

attractions.name: Petroglyph Beach State Historic Site
attractions.season: Year-round but only readable at low tide
attractions.blurb: Roughly 40 prehistoric petroglyphs pecked into beach bedrock north of town, among the densest such sites in Southeast.
attractions.tip: Check the Wrangell tide table - they vanish at high water. Make rubbings only from the boardwalk replicas, never originals.

attractions.name: Anan Wildlife Observatory
attractions.season: Permits required Jul 5-Aug 25; bears follow the pink run
attractions.cost: $30 permit in 2026 plus ~$250-350 guided boat or floatplane
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk and photo blind above Anan Creek falls, where black bears and brown bears fish a huge pink salmon run together.
attractions.tip: Only 60 people a day in peak season and half the permits go to guides - book the guided boat trip, not a bare permit.
attractions.duration_note: full day from Wrangell or Ketchikan

attractions.name: Stikine River & Shakes Glacier jet boat
attractions.season: Jet boats May-Sep; lake ice can block the glacier face
attractions.blurb: Shallow-draft jet boats run the fast, silty Stikine up to iceberg-choked Shakes Lake and the face of Shakes Glacier.
attractions.tip: Iceberg jams sometimes stop boats short of the glacier; operators will tell you honestly the morning of the trip.

attractions.name: Petersburg
attractions.season: Year-round; Little Norway Festival third weekend of May
attractions.blurb: Norwegian-founded fishing town on Mitkof Island; too small for big ships, so it is all working harbor and rosemaling trim.
attractions.tip: No cruise berth means no crowds. Come for LeConte Glacier, the muskeg boardwalks and the best halibut fleet in Southeast.

attractions.name: LeConte Glacier
attractions.season: Boat and flightseeing May-Sep
attractions.blurb: The southernmost tidewater glacier in the northern hemisphere, calving into LeConte Bay about 25 mi east of Petersburg.
attractions.tip: Famous for 'shooters' - bergs that surface explosively. The bay is often so ice-packed that boats stop a mile short.

attractions.name: Wrangell Narrows
attractions.season: Transited year-round; daylight passage is the point
attractions.blurb: 22 mi dogleg channel south of Petersburg, under 300 ft wide in places, marked by more than 60 lit navigation aids.
attractions.tip: Only state ferries and small ships fit through. Time a Columbia or Kennicott leg so you transit in daylight.

attractions.name: Ketchikan
attractions.season: Cruise season May-Sep; year-round town
attractions.blurb: First Alaskan port most ships reach: four downtown berths, the world's largest collection of standing totem poles, 150 in of rain.
attractions.tip: Four ships can dock at once and downtown gridlocks 9am-2pm; the totem parks north and south of town are the escape.
attractions.duration_note: a ship day, or 2 days independent

attractions.name: Creek Street
attractions.season: Boardwalk year-round; shops and museum May-Sep
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk of stilt houses over Ketchikan Creek, the 1903-1954 red-light district, now shops and the Dolly's House museum.
attractions.tip: Late July to September salmon pack the creek below the boardwalk. The $5 funicular to Cape Fox Lodge buys the best view.

attractions.name: Totem Bight State Historical Park
attractions.season: Grounds open year-round, daylight hours
attractions.blurb: Fourteen recarved totems and a painted clan house on a short forest path to a Tongass Narrows point, 10 mi north of town.
attractions.tip: The best-sited totem park in Southeast, and quieter than Saxman. Pairs naturally with the airport-side highway drive.

attractions.name: Saxman Native Village & Totem Park
attractions.season: Poles year-round; performances on ship days May-Sep
attractions.cost: Free to walk; ~$40 show and carving-shed tour
attractions.blurb: Tlingit village 2.5 mi south of Ketchikan with 25+ totems relocated in the 1930s, a clan house and a working carving shed.
attractions.tip: The poles are outdoors and free. The dance performance and carver talk only run when cruise ships are in port.

attractions.name: Totem Heritage Center
attractions.season: Daily May-Sep; limited days in winter
attractions.blurb: Climate-controlled hall of 33 unrestored 19th-century totem poles recovered from abandoned Tlingit and Haida village sites.
attractions.tip: The only place to see original weathered, unpainted poles. Visit here before the outdoor replica parks, not after.

attractions.name: Southeast Alaska Discovery Center
attractions.season: Daily May-Sep; Tue-Sat in winter
attractions.blurb: Forest Service interpretive center on Main Street: rainforest diorama, Tongass ecology, and a public-lands trip-planning desk.
attractions.tip: Best single place to get real Tongass cabin and trail information plus current Anan and Misty Fjords conditions.

attractions.name: Misty Fjords National Monument
attractions.season: Floatplanes and boats May-Sep, weather permitting
attractions.blurb: 2.3-million-acre wilderness of 3,000 ft granite walls and hanging waterfalls; the standard visit is a floatplane water landing.
attractions.tip: 'Misty' is not a marketing word. Boat-and-fly combos hedge the weather far better than a fly-only booking.
attractions.duration_note: 2 hr flightseeing to full-day boat combo

attractions.name: New Eddystone Rock
attractions.season: Seen on Misty Fjords tours May-Sep
attractions.blurb: 237 ft basalt pillar standing alone in Behm Canal, the eroded neck of a volcanic vent, named by Vancouver in 1793.
attractions.tip: Every Misty Fjords boat passes it and some land on the sand spit beside it at low tide for a beach walk.
attractions.duration_note: a stop on a Misty tour

attractions.name: Deer Mountain Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly Jun-Oct
attractions.blurb: Steep 2.5 mi climb from the edge of downtown to a 3,001 ft summit over Tongass Narrows, with a free shelter on the ridge.
attractions.tip: Trailhead is a 20-minute walk from the cruise berths - the only real hike you can do on a ship day without a vehicle.

attractions.name: Ketchikan International Airport & airport ferry
attractions.season: Year-round, every 15-30 minutes
attractions.blurb: Ketchikan's runway sits on Gravina Island, so every arrival takes a 5-minute vehicle ferry to reach town. No bridge exists.
attractions.tip: This is the famous 'bridge to nowhere' crossing. Allow 45 minutes door-to-gate, more when a ship is unloading.

attractions.name: Alaska Marine Highway - Inside Passage mainline
attractions.season: Summer schedule May 1-Sep 30, 2026; Columbia runs Bellingham-Skagway weekly
attractions.cost: ~$400 walk-on Bellingham to Juneau; cabins extra
attractions.blurb: State ferries link Bellingham WA to Skagway, calling at towns cruise ships skip; cabins optional, solarium sleeping allowed.
attractions.tip: No Prince Rupert BC service and no cross-gulf Juneau-Whittier sailings in 2026 - plan Southeast as a closed loop.

attractions.name: Metlakatla
attractions.season: Ferry and floatplane year-round; tours summer
attractions.blurb: Alaska's only Indian reserve: a Tsimshian community founded in 1887 with a longhouse, dance group and cannery history.
attractions.tip: Day-trippable on the Metlakatla ferry from Ketchikan, but arrange the visit through the community tourism office first.

attractions.name: El Capitan Cave
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Free Forest Service tours late May to early Sep
attractions.cost: Free tour; reserve two days ahead
attractions.blurb: Alaska's longest known cave, about two miles of limestone passage; guided walks go 500 ft in past Pleistocene bone sites.
attractions.tip: 367 steps to the entrance and no children under seven. Book via Thorne Bay Ranger District, 907-828-3304, two days out.

attractions.name: Klawock Totem Park
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Open year-round, daylight hours
attractions.blurb: Twenty-one totems on a hill above Klawock's harbor - the largest totem park in Alaska, mostly moved from old Tuxekan.
attractions.tip: Always open and free; the carving shed below sometimes has work in progress. Good stop driving Hollis to Craig.

attractions.name: Hollis Ferry Terminal
attractions.region: Southeast Alaska - Prince of Wales Island
attractions.season: Daily Ketchikan-Hollis sailings year-round
attractions.blurb: The three-hour Inter-Island Ferry from Ketchikan lands here; it is the only vehicle route onto Prince of Wales Island.
attractions.tip: One sailing each way per day makes a POW day trip impossible - plan an overnight and pre-book a rental car in Klawock.

attractions.name: Pack Creek Bear Viewing Area
attractions.aka: Stan Price State Wildlife Sanctuary, Admiralty Island NM
attractions.season: Permits required Jun 1-Sep 10; peak Jul 5-Aug 25
attractions.blurb: Brown-bear viewing on Seymour Canal, 40 mi south of Juneau, on an island with roughly one bear per square mile.
attractions.tip: Only 24 permits a day and half are held for guides. Permits open in February - the guided floatplane day is the realistic route.

attractions.name: Tenakee Springs
attractions.season: Bathhouse year-round; ferry roughly twice a week
attractions.blurb: One-street, car-free village on Chichagof Island built around a 106F concrete soaking bath, with posted men's and women's hours.
attractions.tip: Reach it on the AMHS LeConte or Alaska Seaplanes from Juneau; bring food, the single store keeps short hours.

attractions.name: Baranof Warm Springs
attractions.season: Usable year-round; access is floatplane or boat only
attractions.blurb: Boardwalk hamlet on Baranof's east side: three bathhouse tubs on the dock plus wild rock pools beside a 100 ft waterfall.
attractions.tip: No scheduled service - charter a floatplane from Sitka, about 35 min. The upper natural pools are far better than the tubs.

attractions.name: Fish Creek Wildlife Observation Site
attractions.season: Bears mid-Jul to early Sep; site staffed in season
attractions.blurb: Forest Service viewing platform 4 mi north of Hyder over a chum and pink creek - road-accessible brown and black bears.
attractions.tip: Reached through Stewart BC on Hwy 37A, so bring a passport. Continue 20 mi of gravel for the Salmon Glacier overlook.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 hours, best early morning or evening

attractions.name: Hubbard Glacier
attractions.season: Ships approach Jun-Aug; ice conditions decide how close
attractions.blurb: Six-mile-wide, 400 ft calving face at the head of Yakutat Bay - North America's largest tidewater glacier, and still advancing.
attractions.tip: Ships hold a mile or two off for 1-2 hours. It is the coldest deck time of an Alaska cruise even in July, so layer up.

attractions.name: Yakutat
attractions.season: Surf and steelhead spring and fall; salmon Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: Isolated Gulf-coast town of about 600 between Southeast and Southcentral: steelhead rivers, Hubbard Glacier, real surf breaks.
attractions.tip: Jet service is the Alaska Airlines milk run. There are ~10 mi of road and no rental fleet, so arrange a lodge vehicle.
